title: War in Iran: Europe's complicit silence on international law
subhed: European reluctance to condemn the Israeli-American intervention in Iran flies in the face of the continent's values. No one can believe that Benjamin Netanyahu and Donald Trump's goal is to defend the interests of the Iranian people.

title: Macron calls for 'action' on Lebanon but faces Israel's refusal
subhed: France seeks to replicate the situation that occurred in Lebanon at the end of 2024, in which a ceasefire was brokered between Israel and Hezbollah.

title: Trump says war on Iran will end 'very soon,' calls conflict 'very complete'
subhed: The comments helped lift markets and ease energy fears even as details on any ceasefire remain vague.

title: Human Rights Watch accuses Israel of 'unlawfully' using white phosphorus in new Lebanon attacks
subhed: The NGO released a report on Monday stating that verified images showed airburst white phosphorus munitions being deployed over a residential town in southern Lebanon. White phosphorus can be used as an incendiary weapon and can cause horrific burns, respiratory damage, organ failure and death.

title: One week before France's local elections, electorate leans right, poll shows
subhed: With environmental issues taking a back seat, security issues coming to the fore and a clear increase in support for the far-right Rassemblement National, the survey conducted by Ipsos BVA for Le Monde, Cevipof and the Jean Jaurès Foundation paints an unprecedented picture of the French political landscape.

subhed: Many Iranians in France hope for regime change back home. However, those in favor of democracy are losing ground to supporters of a return to the monarchy, led by the son of Iran's former shah.

title: What the Greek myth of Pandora reveals about the deep-seated fear of women
subhed: By opening a jar entrusted to her by the gods, Pandora becomes the instrument of their vengeance against rebellious humans and unleashes a torrent of plagues upon her fellow mortals. This misogynistic myth seems to contain all the resentments one half of humanity harbors against the other.

title: Tracey Emin has art in her blood: 'Each of her works is both testimony and proof that she survived and has been able to give form to trauma'
subhed: A rebellious and accomplished figure on the British art scene, Emin, who has been affected by illness, has returned to live in Margate, the seaside resort where she grew up. The artist explains how she is developing a microcosm of galleries, schools and studios there, aimed at fostering a new generation of creators. She also looks back on her chaotic career, as the Tate Modern dedicates a retrospective to her.
